items.q: What happened in the Russian Revolution of 1917?
items.correct: There were two revolutions: February overthrew the Tsar and installed the Provisional Government; October saw the Bolsheviks seize power from that government.
items.misconception: The Bolsheviks overthrew the Tsar in 1917.
items.random_wrong: Lenin personally led the storming of the Winter Palace.
items.why: Students collapse 1917 into one event. The Tsar abdicated in February under pressure from liberals and soldiers — the Bolsheviks took power eight months later from a republican government.

items.q: What were the Wars of the Roses really about?
items.correct: Dynastic conflict between rival branches of the Plantagenets (Lancaster and York) over the throne, intensified by weak kingship under Henry VI and noble factionalism.
items.misconception: The whole country was at war for thirty continuous years.
items.random_wrong: They were a religious war between Catholics and Protestants.
items.why: Fighting was sporadic and most of England saw no battles; ordinary life carried on. Students imagine total civil war; in reality, it was an aristocratic dispute fought in short campaigns.

items.q: Why were the Whitechapel murders never solved?
items.correct: Lack of forensic techniques, no fingerprinting or DNA, conflicting witness accounts, jurisdictional confusion between forces, and possibly police incompetence and media interference.
items.misconception: The police failed to solve the murders because they didn't try hard enough.
items.random_wrong: The Ripper was caught but his name was kept secret to protect the Royal Family.
items.why: The police investigated heavily but lacked tools we take for granted. Royal conspiracy theories are unsupported; the limitation was technological and institutional.

items.q: Who led the Bolsheviks in October 1917?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Lenin. “Stalin” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
The answer examiners want is: NEP. “Five-Year Plan” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In what year did the October Revolution occur?
items.why: The real figure is 1917 AD. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
